author.name: Kevan Lin
author.headline: Turning user research, SQL, and AI-built prototypes into products people actually use

posts.content:
I don’t know about you but I’m finding it much easier to design and iterate quicker when I’ve fired up Cursor and built my initial concept in the browser. Honestly it’s no different than my design practice of starting in the browser generally. AI has just sped up the process by 10x. My question though - why are we round tripping our design work back into Figma (assuming you started there to begin with)? 

Admittedly, I should figure out a way to get my prototype back to Figma using a MCP server or something. But right now I’m iterating on the prototype based on product/engineering/user feedback while also updating the Figma file for internal commenting. Is this what your process looks like now too? How have you cut down on time with the “production” work of updating Figma files?

ai.title: AI-powered browser prototyping with Cursor speeds up design iteration 10x
ai.summary: Designer shares workflow of starting design concepts directly in browser using Cursor AI, which speeds up iteration by 10x compared to traditional Figma-first approach. Currently iterating on prototypes based on product/engineering/user feedback without round-tripping to Figma, though considering MCP server integration for syncing.
ai.why_important: Demonstrates practical shift from design tools to code-first workflows, questioning the necessity of traditional design handoff processes.
ai.learnings: Browser-first prototyping with AI coding assistants can eliminate the Figma-to-code translation step while maintaining rapid iteration.

author.name: Punit Chawla
author.headline: UX/UI Designer | Speaker at TedX, IIT, Google & More | 170K+ on Youtube | Design Content Creator & Author

posts.content:
Figma just launched its most useful AI feature yet! 😍  You can now import your design system/library into Figma Make and teach the AI your design style and principles! 🎉

Learn more about this update - https://lnkd.in/empwZRVB

#uidesign #design #uxdesign #ux #ui #productdesign #appdesign #webdesign #learndesign #figma #figmatutorial #figmatips #designtips #figmamake #figmaai #ai #aidesign

ai.title: Figma Make now imports design systems to teach AI your style and principles
ai.summary: Figma launched a feature allowing designers to import their design system or library into Figma Make, teaching the AI their specific design style and principles. This enables AI-generated designs that align with existing brand guidelines and component libraries.
ai.why_important: Bridges the gap between AI design generation and real-world design system consistency, making AI tools more practical for production work.
ai.learnings: You can now feed your existing design libraries into Figma's AI to maintain brand consistency in AI-generated work.

author.name: David Roberts
author.headline: Building AI Ads and Agents (Let’s connect)

posts.content:
I built an AI system that creates luxury real estate listing videos for $12 of credits (just from a Zillow link).

The average agent pays $200–$800 per property for professional video production. Sell this output to realtors and agents and make $$$.

Here's how it works:

→ Drop listing photos into Calico AI Listing Generator and paste the property URL
→ The AI researches comps, neighborhood, and the most marketable features
→ It writes a voiceover script optimized for your target video length
→ Select an AI voice actor — multiple voices and dialects available
→ Custom background music generated from a single text prompt
→ Every photo transforms into a cinematic 4-second video clip
→ Auto-captioned, combined, and ready to publish. No editing required.

The result: every listing gets a professional video tour, not just the $800K+ properties that justify a videographer.

35% more buyer inquiries on listings using these videos.

Today, the agents using this have an unfair advantage.

Tomorrow, every agent will have it and it'll be table stakes.

These windows of opportunity always only last so long...

Comment "LISTING" and I'll send you a full video tutorial breaking down how to do this and the tools I'm using (must be connected so I can DM you).

ai.title: AI video workflow creates luxury real estate listings from Zillow links for $12
ai.summary: Built system using Calico AI that converts Zillow listing photos into professional real estate videos for $12 vs. $200-800 traditional cost. Process: uploads photos, pastes URL, AI researches comps/neighborhood, writes optimized voiceover script, generates custom music, and selects from multiple AI voice actors.
ai.why_important: Shows complete automated video production workflow with specific cost savings and concrete steps, making professional video accessible to individual agents.
ai.learnings: Calico AI can scrape property data, generate contextual scripts, and produce complete listing videos by combining listing photos with AI-generated voiceover and music.

author.name: Eugenio Zabell
author.headline: AI & Creative Strategy // Powering the next generation of E-commerce brands through viral creatives and new client acquisition.

posts.content:
Formula 1 teams spend $100,000+ on production films.

I made one in 4 minutes.

In 2026, cinematic video content doesn't require a massive budget and a production crew.

I've been testing an AI workflow that generates video ads that look like they cost thousands to produce - and I documented the exact process.

What's inside:

→ 7 copy-and-paste prompts for high-converting video ads
→ The exact AI platforms to use and which ones to combine
→ How to generate images and animate them seamlessly
→ The workflow sequence that gets it done in minutes
→ Real examples with prompts that drove results

This is the same process I use to create ads for clients without touching a camera.

Without production crews, Adobe Suite or agency retainers.

Just the right prompts in the right sequence.

The results:

• Cinematic video content without filming
• Multiple ad variations in minutes, not weeks
• Zero video editing skills required
• Works for any brand in any category

Want the full breakdown with all 7 prompts?

Comment "PROMPTS" and I'll send it over.

♻️ Repost for priority access

ai.title: 7-prompt workflow generates Formula 1 production-quality ads in 4 minutes
ai.summary: Documented complete workflow for creating cinematic video ads that replicate $100K+ production quality. Includes 7 specific prompts, platform combinations for image generation and animation, and workflow sequence that produces results in minutes.
ai.why_important: Provides reproducible workflow for high-end video ad creation, demonstrating specific prompt strategies rather than vague AI claims.
ai.learnings: Combining specific AI platforms with optimized prompt sequences can replicate high-budget production aesthetics at minimal cost.
